title: The Farm: Hop-Picking (23rd August 2020)

main_route.excerpt: Tinder, Simon + Eve set off at 4.30. for hop picking at the farm. We stayed from Sunday to Saturday.
main_route.notes: Unnamed hop farm; identified as Kate and Jamie's farm near Bromyard, Herefordshire, from other diary entries (e.g. 2004-october-trimpley-the-farm and 1995-bromyard-kate-jamie-s-farm). Two stays recorded: Sun 23 Aug to Sat 29 Aug, then again Sun 30 Aug to Mon 31 Aug (next to the croquet lawn behind the yew hedge). Location approximate.

main_route.excerpt: We are heading home now to go to a socially distanced street party with our neighbours-to-be at George Rd.

unresolved.original_wording: Harry stood on a conveyor belt when Henry switched on
unresolved.issue: Harry and Henry are workers at the farm (not family members); mentioned by first name only, cannot locate further.
unresolved.excerpt: Harry stood on a conveyor belt when Henry switched on + nearly had his leg dragged in by a machine.

unresolved.issue: Worker at the farm; surname illegible in transcription. Not a place.
unresolved.excerpt: Pavel [illegible] tore skin on his palm. William asked me to do first aid.

summary: Simon, Eve and Tinder made two consecutive working visits to Kate and Jamie's hop farm near Bromyard, Herefordshire in late August 2020: first Sunday 23 to Saturday 29 August for the main hop harvest, then returning on Sunday 30 August for a further two nights (next to the croquet lawn behind the yew hedge) before heading home to Birmingham on about 1 September. The diary records hop-machine repairs, a near-accident on a conveyor belt, first aid, and time spent with grandchildren.
extraction_notes: Single diary page covering two back-to-back farm stays. The farm is not named in this entry but is identified as Kate and Jamie's farm near Bromyard, Herefordshire, from cross-references to earlier trips (1995-bromyard-kate-jamie-s-farm, 2004-october-trimpley-the-farm, 2017-farm-hop-barn). The first stay ran Sun 23 to Sat 29 Aug; the second ran Sun 30 Aug to Mon 31 Aug, with the family heading home on approximately 1 Sep 2020. Westfield Rd and George Rd are Birmingham addresses (a pending house move) — not route stops. William is mentioned only in passing as asking Eve to do first aid; he is not a traveller on this trip. No crossings. date_end set to 2020-09-01 as the most probable return date (after Monday night at the farm).
